behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior across directories (recursive watch semantics and OS event delivery). A unit/integration fake can simulate subdirectory events and miss real watcher misconfiguration/platform behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text is purely filesystem/daemon, with no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is explicitly about deduplication logic for same path + time window; this is local algorithm/state and can fail without any real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected System because behavior does not require real filesystem events; rejected higher AI levels because no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a test clock, submit path P, then submit path P again within the configured window and assert second request is rejected; advance clock beyond window and assert a new request for P is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events; mocks hide real event burst patterns and timing. Minimum boundary is real watcher + real filesystem write behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher on a temp directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename or buffered writes), capture pipeline invocations, and assert only one processing execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS/filesystem lifecycle boundary (directory inode removal/recreation) plus watcher re-subscription behavior; in-process fakes can miss real detach/reattach failure modes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is daemon/watcher resilience only, no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real temp directory, delete the watched directory while daemon stays up, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between real file creation metadata and asynchronous arrival handling; mocks can force ideal timestamps/order and miss real timestamp precision/event-order issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML content decision exists.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create multiple files in rapid succession, record each file's creation timestamp from filesystem metadata, then assert processing order matches timestamp order (with defined tie-breaker expectation if timestamps are equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in real write/read race timing and OS event timing; fake writers or mocked watchers often hide partial-write windows. Real filesystem behavior is required to catch premature pickup bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory, begin writing a large file in chunks with delays so arrival event occurs before completion, assert daemon does not process while file is incomplete, then assert processing occurs once final write/close completes.
